---
title: "Chapter 13 - Kshetra-Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञविभागयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-12.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-14.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-13-1" data-reference="13.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-1">13.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I wish to know <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">purusha</span>, the field and the knower of the field, and knowledge and what is to be known, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." title="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="keshava">Keshava</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च ।<br>
एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-2" data-reference="13.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-2">13.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This body, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, is called the field; and the one who knows it, those who know call the knower of the field.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते ।<br>
एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-3" data-reference="13.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-3">13.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know me too, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>, as the knower of the field in all fields; and the knowledge of the field and its knower—that, I hold, is true knowledge.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत ।<br>
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-4" data-reference="13.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-4">13.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">What that field is, of what kind, with what changes, whence it arises, and who he is and what his powers are—hear this from me in brief.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् ।<br>
स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श‍ृणु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-5" data-reference="13.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-5">13.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This has been sung by seers in many ways, in various hymns, and in the well-reasoned, definitive words of the aphorisms on <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक् ।<br>
ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितैः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-6" data-reference="13.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-6">13.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The great elements, the ego, the understanding, and the unmanifest; the ten senses and the one mind, and the five fields of sense;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च ।<br>
इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-7" data-reference="13.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-7">13.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">desire and aversion, pleasure and pain, the bodily whole, awareness, and steadfastness—this, in brief, is the field together with its changes.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतना धृतिः ।<br>
एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-8" data-reference="13.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-8">13.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Humility, sincerity, nonviolence, patience, uprightness, service to the teacher, purity, steadiness, and self-restraint;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् ।<br>
आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-9" data-reference="13.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-9">13.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">indifference to the objects of sense, absence of ego, and a clear sight of the evil in birth, death, old age, sickness, and pain;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च ।<br>
जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-10" data-reference="13.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-10">13.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">non-attachment, freedom from clinging to son, wife, home, and the rest, and a constant evenness of mind toward the welcome and the unwelcome;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु ।<br>
नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-11" data-reference="13.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-11">13.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">unswerving devotion to me through undistracted <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, resort to secluded places, and no delight in crowds of people;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी ।<br>
विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-12" data-reference="13.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-12">13.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">constancy in knowledge of the self and insight into the aim of true knowledge—this is declared to be knowledge; what differs from it is ignorance.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् ।<br>
एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-13" data-reference="13.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-13">13.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I shall declare what is to be known, knowing which one gains the deathless: the beginningless supreme <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, said to be neither being nor nonbeing.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते ।<br>
अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-14" data-reference="13.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-14">13.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With hands and feet on every side, with eyes, heads, and faces on every side, with ears on every side, it abides, enfolding all in the world.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् ।<br>
सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-15" data-reference="13.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-15">13.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Shining through the powers of all the senses, yet free of all the senses; unattached, yet bearing all; without <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, yet enjoying the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् ।<br>
असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-16" data-reference="13.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-16">13.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">outside and inside all beings; unmoving, yet moving; too subtle to be known; far away, and yet near.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च ।<br>
सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-17" data-reference="13.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-17">13.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Undivided, yet seeming divided among beings; this is to be known as the sustainer of beings, their devourer and their source.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् ।<br>
भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-18" data-reference="13.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-18">13.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">It is the light even of lights, said to be beyond darkness; it is knowledge, the object of knowledge, to be reached by knowledge, seated in the hearts of all.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते ।<br>
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-19" data-reference="13.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-19">13.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus the field, knowledge, and the knowable have been told in brief; one devoted to me, knowing this, becomes fit for my being.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः ।<br>
मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-20" data-reference="13.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-20">13.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know that both <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">purusha</span> are without beginning; and know that the changes and the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span> are born of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि ।<br>
विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-21" data-reference="13.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-21">13.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">Prakriti</span> is said to be the cause in the working of cause and effect;<br>
<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">purusha</span> is said to be the cause in the experiencing of pleasure and pain.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते ।<br>
पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-22" data-reference="13.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-22">13.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">purusha</span>, seated in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>, experiences the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span> born of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>; attachment to the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span> is the cause of its birth in good and evil wombs.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् ।<br>
कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-23" data-reference="13.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-23">13.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The supreme <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">purusha</span> in this body is also called the witness, the consenter, the bearer, the experiencer, the great lord, and the supreme <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." aria-label="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." title="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." data-glossary-id="atman">Atman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः ।<br>
परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-24" data-reference="13.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-24">13.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who thus knows <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">purusha</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>, together with the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, in whatever state he lives, is not born again.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैः सह ।<br>
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-25" data-reference="13.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-25">13.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Some, by meditation, see the self in the self by the self; others by the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sankhya</span>, and others by the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना ।<br>
अन्ये साङ्ख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-26" data-reference="13.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-26">13.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Others, not knowing this, worship having heard it from others; they too, devoted to what they hear, cross beyond death.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते ।<br>
तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-27" data-reference="13.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-27">13.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever being is born, moving or unmoving, know it to arise from the union of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="kshetra: Field, especially the body or field of experience in Chapter 13." aria-label="kshetra: Field, especially the body or field of experience in Chapter 13." title="kshetra: Field, especially the body or field of experience in Chapter 13." data-glossary-id="kshetra">kshetra</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="kshetrajna: Knower of the field; the conscious principle related to kshetra." aria-label="kshetrajna: Knower of the field; the conscious principle related to kshetra." title="kshetrajna: Knower of the field; the conscious principle related to kshetra." data-glossary-id="kshetrajna">kshetrajna</span>, O best of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharatas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् ।<br>
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-28" data-reference="13.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-28">13.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who sees the supreme lord dwelling alike in all beings, undying amid the dying, truly sees.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् ।<br>
विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-29" data-reference="13.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-29">13.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For seeing the lord present the same everywhere, he does not harm the self by the self, and so reaches the supreme goal.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् ।<br>
न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-30" data-reference="13.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-30">13.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who sees that actions are performed in every way by <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span> alone, and that the self is not the doer, truly sees.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः ।<br>
यः पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-31" data-reference="13.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-31">13.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When he sees the separate states of beings as resting in the One, and their spreading forth from that alone, then he attains <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति ।<br>
तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-32" data-reference="13.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-32">13.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Beginningless and without <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, this supreme self, imperishable, though seated in the body, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, neither acts nor is tainted.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः ।<br>
शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-33" data-reference="13.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-33">13.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As all-pervading space, being subtle, is not tainted, so the self, seated everywhere in the body, is not tainted.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते ।<br>
सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-34" data-reference="13.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-34">13.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As the one sun lights up this whole world, so the knower of the field lights up the whole field, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः ।<br>
क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-13-35" data-reference="13.35">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-13-35">13.35</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who, with the eye of knowledge, thus discern the difference between <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="kshetra: Field, especially the body or field of experience in Chapter 13." aria-label="kshetra: Field, especially the body or field of experience in Chapter 13." title="kshetra: Field, especially the body or field of experience in Chapter 13." data-glossary-id="kshetra">kshetra</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="kshetrajna: Knower of the field; the conscious principle related to kshetra." aria-label="kshetrajna: Knower of the field; the conscious principle related to kshetra." title="kshetrajna: Knower of the field; the conscious principle related to kshetra." data-glossary-id="kshetrajna">kshetrajna</span>, and the freeing of beings from <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>, reach the Supreme.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा ।<br>
भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
